scope: J group round three postmatch model backtest only: Algeria vs Austria; Jordan vs Argentina. No betting advice.

prediction_markdown: J group single-match markdown: Algeria vs Austria quantitative prediction, 2026-06-28
J group single-match markdown: Jordan vs Argentina quantitative prediction, 2026-06-28

actual_result.event_note: External reports describe a 3-3 draw with late stoppage-time goals and both teams advancing.

market_calibration_error.calibration_read: market_draw_signal_helped; model underweighted draw relative to market by about 9.0 pp
market_calibration_error.handicap_actual_bucket: Algeria +1 win because real match was draw
market_calibration_error.handicap_calibration_read: market priced Algeria +1 protection higher than model; actual landed inside that protection bucket

postmatch_error.summary: The published probability kept draw at 33.0%, but the primary direction remained Austria by a narrow margin. Actual 3-3 exposed a correct draw warning with a badly missed event/score shape.

factor_error.market_signal: Market draw no-vig probability was about 42.0%, much higher than model draw 33.0%; market calibration should have carried more draw-floor weight.
factor_error.tactical_signal: High press and transition danger were captured, but late set-piece/second-ball volatility was capped too tightly.
factor_error.player_state_signal: Algeria attacking individual quality and Austria set-piece depth both mattered more in late-game chaos than the low-event base assumed.

score_tail_error.diagnosis: 3-3 sat far outside Top5 and carried only about 0.8% matrix mass. The model underweighted six-goal mutual-qualification chaos and stoppage-time exchange paths.

draw_floor_error.diagnosis: Draw risk was identified but not enough to become the primary published direction despite market and qualification incentives. This is less a missing draw floor than an underweighted draw-floor plus high-event draw branch.

third_round_context_error.diagnosis: The model treated draw incentives mostly as low-event control. In reality the same incentive coexisted with late group-table volatility, causing a high-event draw rather than a sterile draw.

actual_result.event_note: External report records Argentina 3-1 Jordan; Argentina won with a rotated/managed context but retained scoring ceiling.

market_calibration_error.calibration_read: ordinary 1X2 was missing in the released same-source packet, so 1X2 market calibration could not be tested
market_calibration_error.handicap_actual_bucket: Jordan +2 draw because Argentina won by exactly two goals
market_calibration_error.handicap_calibration_read: handicap margin market was close to the model exact two-goal bucket and the actual result hit that bucket

postmatch_error.summary: Argentina win direction hit clearly, but 1-3 missed Top5 and the model leaned too low on total goals and BTTS.

factor_error.market_signal: Ordinary 1X2 market was missing, but handicap +2 draw was close to model and actual margin; the margin layer was more informative than the totals layer.
factor_error.tactical_signal: Jordan low block and Argentina rotation were recognized, but Argentina elite depth and set-piece/substitution ceiling were over-discounted.
factor_error.player_state_signal: Jordan still had enough transition/set-piece quality for one goal, while Argentina rotation did not remove the third-goal ceiling.

score_tail_error.diagnosis: 1-3 carried about 5.6% matrix mass but fell outside Top5. The model had Argentina 0-3 in Top3 and 1-2 in Top5, but did not connect Jordan scoring with Argentina still reaching three.

draw_floor_error.diagnosis: The draw floor was not the failure; if anything the red-team/rotation layer held draw at 24.0% while actual match confirmed Argentina superiority.

third_round_context_error.diagnosis: The model correctly reduced runaway blowout risk versus a fully motivated favorite, but over-applied rotation deceleration and underweighted elite bench/set-piece scoring.

player_state_backtest_link.status_reason: Data collector postmatch packet and 2026-06-28 player_state/member-table updates were not present at model runtime; four player_state files predate this matchday.
missing_inputs: official full lineups and minutes for both J group matches
substitution times and card/event log normalized by data thread
postmatch internal_match_rating_1_5 entries for Algeria, Austria, Jordan, Argentina
member-table append entries for the 2026-06-28 matches

common_diagnosis.direction_layer: One primary direction hit and one narrow primary miss. Algeria-Austria shows that draw warning existed but was not strong enough; Jordan-Argentina shows favorite direction remained robust despite rotation.
common_diagnosis.score_layer: Both exact scores missed Top5. The system still underweights high-event third-round score branches when late incentives or elite depth re-open a controlled game.
common_diagnosis.totals_layer: Both matches landed over 2.5 and over 3.5 while model leaned under. The under bias in final group-round control scripts needs a confidence cap.
common_diagnosis.market_layer: Market draw signal helped in Algeria-Austria; ordinary 1X2 was missing for Jordan-Argentina, while +2 handicap draw was informative for the exact two-goal margin.

parameter_updates.current_behavior: Draw incentive is present but can remain secondary when base strength slightly favors one side.
parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.02 to +0.04 draw probability when draw materially benefits both teams or market draw no-vig exceeds model by at least 0.07.
parameter_updates.cap: single parameter shift <= 0.04; do not force draw if one side must win by multi-goal margin.
parameter_updates.reason: Algeria-Austria finished draw and market draw signal was materially higher than the model.

parameter_updates.current_behavior: Third-round context often lowers tempo and total goals.
parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.03 to +0.06 redistributed from 0-0/1-0/0-1/1-1 cluster into 3+ total and 4+ total tail when simultaneous table state can flip late.
parameter_updates.cap: combined pre-red-team shift <= 0.10 across context, totals, and tail layers.
parameter_updates.reason: Algeria-Austria 3-3 showed late mutual-qualification chaos; Jordan-Argentina 1-3 also beat the under baseline.

parameter_updates.current_behavior: Rotation/qualified-state layer suppresses favorite xG and 3+ margin tail.
parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.04 to +0.08 to favorite scoring-tail only when squad-depth score >= 80 and elite substitutes/set-piece takers remain available.
parameter_updates.cap: do not raise base win probability by more than 0.03 from this parameter alone.
parameter_updates.reason: Argentina rotation did not prevent a three-goal output; elite depth kept the ceiling alive.

parameter_updates.current_behavior: Clear favorite games can suppress BTTS too aggressively.
parameter_updates.proposed_delta: +0.02 to +0.04 BTTS yes when underdog has transition/set-piece path and favorite rotation lowers defensive continuity.
parameter_updates.cap: BTTS yes floor normally <= 0.42 unless underdog xG >= 0.75.
parameter_updates.reason: Jordan scored despite Argentina control; Algeria also exceeded its scoring mean strongly.

parameter_updates.current_behavior: Under 2.5 can stay above 0.54 in controlled third-round scripts.
parameter_updates.proposed_delta: cap under_2_5 published confidence at 0.58 unless both teams have static draw incentive, low transition pace, and no late table dependency.
parameter_updates.cap: confidence cap, not direct probability inflation.
parameter_updates.reason: Both J matches beat O2.5/O3.5 while model leaned under.

parameter_updates.current_behavior: 1X2 market and handicap market are both treated as calibration notes but not always converted into separate diagnostic channels.
parameter_updates.proposed_delta: separate draw-floor calibration from exact-margin/handicap calibration; never let +2 or +1 handicap replace ordinary 1X2.
parameter_updates.cap: schema/algorithm change; no direct probability shift.
parameter_updates.reason: Algeria-Austria market draw helped 1X2; Jordan-Argentina handicap +2 draw helped margin, while ordinary 1X2 was missing.

quality_notes: No betting advice is included; odds are evaluated only as calibration signals.
Actual scores are externally verified because the local J group postmatch packet was not present during this modeler run.
Player-state linkage remains partial until the data collector normalizes minutes, cards, substitutions, injuries, and internal ratings.
